title: DaVinci Resolve Setup and Project Manager

video.note: Introduces DaVinci Resolve as an all-in-one post-production tool, system expectations, free versus Studio awareness, Resolve pages, Project Manager, new project creation, project backup/export habits, and first workspace orientation.

learningTarget: I can create a DaVinci Resolve project, identify the main Resolve pages, and explain why project organization matters before editing begins.

bellRinger.prompt: What is one problem that can happen if an editor starts cutting video before naming, saving, and organizing the project?

vocabulary.definition: A video post-production application used for editing, visual effects, graphics, and delivery.
The startup area used to create, open, back up, and organize Resolve projects.
The database or location where Resolve stores project files and timelines.
A dedicated workspace inside Resolve, such as Media, Edit, Fusion, or Deliver.
The work that happens after footage is recorded, including editing, sound, graphics, and export.
The visible panels and tools arranged for a specific editing task.

slides.title: Lesson 01 - DaVinci Resolve Setup and Project Manager

assignment.title: Resolve Project Setup Proof
evidenceRequired: Google Drive link to a screenshot of the named Resolve project or Project Manager entry.
Google Drive or Docs link showing four Resolve pages with short purpose notes.
Short reflection explaining why project setup matters before editing.

exitTicket: Which Resolve page or startup area did you use today, and what job does it help an editor do?
